summary: Rounds 1-3’s consumer-side issues are resolved: the trainer now cleanly separates strict truth from heuristic compatibility, anchor detection is leaf-only, long single-token nav labels no longer auto-win as pills, and real `<button>` leaves are capped out of the pill 100-path with regression tests covering the prior cases. I would not keep this at `warn` because of the deferred producer selector issue alone; that is correctly documented as separate scope. I am keeping `warn` because there is still one consumer-side false-positive class: button-like non-native controls such as `div/span[role=\"

issues.description: The new pill cap only applies when `leafTag(region.selector) === 'button'`. Button-like non-native controls still fall through. For a compact `div`/`span` with `detection='r9-aria-role'`, label `Save`, and bounds around `60x28`, `clickable/pill` still scores `100`, `clickable/link` gets `80` (`r9` + compact height), and `clickable/button` only gets `60` because it does not reward `r9-aria-role`. A
issues.suggested_fix: Extend the anti-pill/button-truth rule to button-like ARIA detections, not just native `<button>` leaves. The minimal fix is to cap pill when `region.detection === 'r9-aria-role'` and the region is compact/short-label, or to give `clickable/button` a compensating boost for button-like ARIA controls while avoiding `role='link'` cases if that signal becomes available.

strengths: `trainer-vs-truth.mjs` now separates strict pattern truth from heuristic compatibility, which resolves the earlier circularity concern and makes the reported metrics defensible.
`leafTag()` and `isAnchorSelector()` are now leaf-based, so descendant nodes inside anchors no longer inherit anchor truth by accident.
`clickable/pill` now requires tighter geometry for short alpha labels, which closes the long single-token nav false-positive class.
The new `<button>` cap plus the regression tests in `pattern-library.test.ts` correctly fix the Save/Back/Next/Login cases without disturbing the existing span-based badge cases.
